title: Hydraulic Disc Brake — Four-Wheeler Emergency Stop
content: Hydraulic disc brake converts kinetic energy to heat via friction between caliper pads and rotating disc. Master cylinder multiplies pedal force via Pascal's law. Braking torque T = µ·F_clamp·r_eff·n_pads.
extended_content: ABS (Anti-lock Braking System) modulates hydraulic pressure 10–15 Hz to keep slip ratio 10–20% (maximum friction coefficient). Modern four-wheelers use diagonal split circuits for failsafe. Brake fade at disc temperatures > 400°C. Ventilated discs increase heat dissipation 40%. Caliper piston seal rollback reduces residual drag. Deceleration: a = µg (µ≈0.7 dry asphalt), stopping distance d = v²/(2µg).
formula: T = µ · F_clamp · r_eff · n_pads ;  d_stop = v²/(2·µ·g)
units: T: N·m, F_clamp: N, r_eff: m, d: m, v: m/s
source: Bosch Automotive Handbook, 10th ed.; SAE J2522 Brake Performance Test; Gillespie, Fundamentals of Vehicle Dynamics, SAE 1992
